news.title: BIO 2026: AI, federal policy impacts, and general vibes
news.summary: What were the vibes like at BIO 2026? STAT reporters take you inside the annual industry conference on the latest episode of STATus Report.

news.title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
news.summary: From new hires to departures, promotions and transfers, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

news.title: 988’s LGBTQ+ hotline to relaunch this year. But the group that helped start it might be excluded
news.summary: The Trump administration is moving to restart the specialized LGBTQ+ option for youth who contact the 988 crisis line, but the group that helped pioneer the idea is being shut…

news.title: STAT+: Embryo editing advances reignite ethical debates
news.summary: Cassidy targets 340B, Eli Lilly's mysterious retatrutide patient, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a bill to overhaul the 340B program, a new FDA commissioner candidate, and more
news.summary: And so, another working week will soon draw to a close. Not a moment too soon, yes? This is, you may recall, our treasured signal to daydream about weekend plans.…

news.title: STAT+: European antitrust regulators probe Sanofi for allegedly disparaging a rival flu vaccine
news.summary: The European Commission is investigating Sanofi for allegedly breaching antitrust rules by running a “misleading” campaign about a flu vaccine.

news.title: Supreme Court rules in weedkiller cancer case
news.summary: Dems want to unmask Eli Lilly's obesity drug user, a new genome-editing tool raises ethics questions, and more health news

news.title: What is pulmonary hypertension and why would a new GLP-1 help?
news.summary: A report about unusual access to an experimental obesity drug has raised interest in the condition pulmonary hypertension.

news.title: Opinion: Joseph Fraumeni Jr., pioneering cancer genetics researcher, devoted his life to families like mine
news.summary: “They don't make many like Joe Fraumeni anymore,” writes Lawrence Ingrassia, author of “A Fatal Inheritance.”

news.title: Opinion: ‘The Hot Zone’ led me to work with Ebola patients. Now I have mixed feelings about the book
news.summary: “The reality of Ebola is simultaneously less sensational and far more challenging than most people imagine,” writes Krutika Kuppalli.

news.title: Jails are the frontline in fielding dangerous new type of drug withdrawal
news.summary: Medetomidine-laced opioids cause severe, life-threatening withdrawal in jails. Here's why many facilities remain ill-equipped to treat a growing crisis.

news.title: New ACIP charter broadens criteria for members, calls for review of alternatives to vaccines
news.summary: A new charter for the panel that advises the CDC on vaccine use substantially refocuses the responsibilities of the committee, downplaying its role in recommending the use of new vaccines.

news.title: STAT+: At BIO 2026, industry wrestled with Washington politics, and making AI work better
news.summary: Biotech executives reveal concerns over Chinese biotech, the profitability of AI, and the durability of Trump's drug price moves.

news.title: The mysterious case of Eli Lilly’s obesity drug
news.summary: Who got exclusive access to Eli Lilly's highly anticipated obesity candidate? And why are drugmakers spending so much on acquisitions? Find out on this week's "Readout LOUD."

news.title: STAT+: Cassidy proposes bill to rein in 340B drug discount program
news.summary: Placing new limits on the 340B drug discount program would be a fresh challenge for hospitals as they confront funding cuts.

news.title: As PBM industry shifts, LucyRx and Abarca Health merge to build scale
news.summary: Amid significant shifts in the pharmacy benefit management industry, LucyRx and Abarca Health have revealed plans to merge to build the scale necessary to compete in this changing landscape.

news.title: Clinicians' compensation rose 4.3% across 2025: AMGA
news.summary: The annual survey of 451 medical groups and health systems found signs of "genuine demand expansion" and productivity increases that support about half of the year's compensation growth.

news.title: Weekly Rundown: Innovaccer, AWS ink strategic collaboration; Mount Sinai adopts Signal 1 for AI management
news.summary: Stay up to date on the latest in health tech, digital health and health AI news with this weekly brief.

news.title: Industry Voices—What Pope Leo’s AI encyclical could mean for healthcare: 3 key takeaways for leaders
news.summary: The papal letter’s guidance on artificial intelligence can provide competitive, legal, reputational and financial benefits to healthcare organizations, faith-based and otherwise, legal experts from Epstein Becker Green write.

news.title: Cassidy's new plan to overhaul 340B: Rebates, contract pharmacy limits and more changes
news.summary: A legislative discussion draft shared Thursday by the Senate HELP chairman includes rebates, contract pharmacy limits, narrowed definitions and other proposals.

news.title: With Medicare's GLP-1 'bridge' coming soon, Walmart expands weight management supports for consumers
news.summary: Medicare's GLP-1 Bridge program goes into effect next week, so Walmart is rolling out new supports aimed at helping beneficiaries understand their weight management options.

news.title: Democrats to propose bill capping out-of-pocket costs for Medicare enrollees
news.summary: Sen. Ron Wyden and 14 Democratic co-sponsors plan to introduce legislation Thursday to cap consumers’ potential out-of-pocket costs in traditional Medicare, resurfacing a long-running debate over why the program doesn’t limit beneficiary spending.

news.title: Oracle partners with Theator to expand AI-powered surgical documentation to operating rooms
news.summary: Oracle Health, owned by tech giant Oracle after it acquired EHR company Cerner in 2022, is ramping up its AI capabilities while also expanding its AI ecosystem through partnerships.

news.title: Judge blocks narrowed definition of 'professional degree' for federal student loan caps
news.summary: Plaintiff provider associations cheered the decision, as the rule's definition would have barred advanced practice students from a higher borrowing limit.

news.title: Upside secures $20M series A to expand healthcare-focused housing platform
news.summary: The company plans to use the funding simultaneously across Medicaid, Medicare Advantage and employer-sponsored markets.

news.title: Fierce Healthcare Layoff Tracker—St. Charles cuts 45 leadership roles; MU Health Care lays off 74
news.summary: Stick with this tracker for the latest layoff and workforce updates from across healthcare.

news.title: TigerConnect expands AI-driven platform with new scheduling capabilities
news.summary: The new platform offers real-time AI-generated gap detection and intelligent assignment logic for schedule balancing.

news.title: Epicrispr gene silencer is 1st drug to boost muscle in form of muscular dystrophy, biotech says
news.summary: Epicrispr Biotechnologies has used its gene silencing tech to boost muscle mass in three patients with facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy, a clinical first for the debilitating disease with no approved treatments.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: Biogen sheds Apellis R&D roles; ADC Tx slims workforce
news.summary: As always, if you know of layoffs occurring at a biotech, please reach out to the Fierce Biotech editorial team.

news.title: Biogen ends Apellis research programs, cuts ‘small number’ of roles after $5.6B buyout
news.summary: Biogen isn’t wasting any time integrating its multibillion-dollar purchase of Apellis, revealing plans to part ways with some of the biotech’s staffers and halt work on most of its research programs.

news.title: The deal before the deal
news.summary: Before a biotech deal gets signed, companies are already being tested by investors, pharma partners and their own pipelines. This episode looks at the pressure, timing and hard choices shaping what makes it to the table.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker '26: Lycia raises $75M; Oblenio reels in $62M
news.summary: The Fierce Biotech Fundraising Tracker records venture capital rounds of $50 million or more secured by biotechs.

news.title: Moderna enters in vivo CAR-T, granting autoimmune asset ‘007’ license to kill pathogenic cells
news.summary: Moderna has unveiled its first in vivo CAR-T program. Building on other groups’ ex vivo CAR-T data, the company plans to start clinical development of an off-the-shelf autoimmune disease prospect, dubbed “007,” next year.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—EMD Serono taps in new commercial strategy head
news.summary: EMD Serono, Merck KGaA’s North American healthcare arm, has promoted from within to replace its retiring head of global commercial strategy, Andrew Paterson. Taking the commercial wheel is Miguel Fernández Alcalde, who’s spent over a decade at the company.

news.title: Cardiac medtech HeartBeam drops CEO in restructuring aimed at global ECG platform push
news.summary: HeartBeam, the maker of a cable-free ECG system, moved CEO Robert Eno into a consulting role as part of a corporate restructuring aimed at expanding the company’s global market reach.

news.title: How iBio aims to stand out in obesity by targeting ‘both sides of the equation’ for antibody discovery
news.summary: Upon finally reaching the clinic, iBio feels vindicated about the artificial intelligence-powered discovery platform it picked up in 2022.

news.title: Fosun Pharma plots global push beyond oncology, fueled by multi-asset Alzheimer’s strategy
news.summary: In a recent interview with Fierce, Fosun Pharma co-president, Xinli Wang, M.D., Ph.D., detailed how the company leveraged two recent deals to quickly build up its portfolio in Alzheimer’s disease as part of a multi-step roadmap to establish its independent capabilities in the U.S.

news.title: BIO 2026: How a gut feeling transformed cancer treatment forever
news.summary: Dr. Israel Lowy remembers the days when he and his oncology immunotherapy colleagues were assigned tables in the distant east wing of the American Society of Clinical Oncology conference in Chicago. They would watch aficionados walk by, scoffing at these iconoclastic researchers pushing an alternative way to treat cancer.

news.title: Otsuka posts phase 3b ADHD trial win ahead of looming FDA approval decision
news.summary: A phase 3b trial of Otsuka’s attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder prospect has hit its primary endpoint, strengthening the drugmaker’s hand weeks before a potential U.S. launch of the therapy.

news.title: DeepMind 研究科学家：广告没人看了，互联网上AI的流量已经超过了真人
news.summary: 全世界的智能体几乎都在用 GPT 和 Claude 那几个模型，一旦它们犯错，全网 AI 将在同一秒迎来大崩溃。

news.title: 抢体脂秤、AI做「搭子」，第一批网友冲向阿福减重
news.summary: 普通人减肥不用焦虑了。

news.title: 氪星晚报 ｜阿福“科学减重1亿斤”行动正式上线；B站：预计到明年，视频播客日均播放时长有望达到3亿分钟
news.summary: 高考出分三日，千问志愿大模型为考生生成500万份志愿报告

papers.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
papers.summary: Multi-model LLM systems such as routing, voting, cascades, fusion, and mixture-of-agents are used to beat single-model accuracy. We show that their gain is capped by a quantity the field rarely reports. For any policy whose output is one member model answer, accuracy cannot exceed one minus beta, where beta is the rate at which every model is wrong on the same query. In contrast, the usual diagnostic, average pairwise error correlation rho, cannot identify beta: error laws with identical marginals and pairwise correlations can have different all-wrong rates. A Clopper-Pearson bound on beta giv

papers.title: How Post-Training Shapes Biological Reasoning Models
papers.summary: Scientific reasoning models for biology combine language models with foundation models trained on multimodal biological data, including DNA, RNA, and proteins. These models are built through post-training, yet how each stage shapes reasoning and generalization remains poorly understood. We study when post-training improves performance and when it induces over-specialization. Across genomics, transcriptomics, and proteins, we train and evaluate more than 100 biological reasoning models under controlled variation in backbone, continued pre-training (CPT), supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and reinfo

papers.title: OpenBioRQ: Unsolved Biomedical Research Questions for Agents
papers.summary: A working citation looks like proof -- but the fact that a link resolves does not mean the cited paper supports the claim. I find that current agentic models rarely fabricate citations (over 99% resolve), yet roughly 15.9% link to the wrong paper. Existing benchmarks miss this failure mode: when a question has a fixed answer key, a model can reproduce the expected source from that key rather than independently verifying that the source supports the claim. I introduce \openbiorq{}, a retrieval-grounded agentic benchmark of 12{,}553 unsolved biomedical research questions across 12 domains that t

papers.title: Plans Don't Persist: Why Context Management Is Load Bearing for LLM Agents
papers.summary: Long-horizon agents depend on context management: systems compress, summarize, and evict old tokens so tasks can continue beyond finite windows. That is safe only when dropped information is no longer needed or has been internalized. Plans are the stress case: they are written early, used for many steps, and first to be evicted. We introduce replay pairing, a diagnostic that runs the same trajectory with and without the plan in history and measures hidden-state cosine distance. On Llama-3.1-70B, plan signal spikes to 0.453 one step after the plan, then falls 4.1x in a single action-observation

papers.title: EBench: Elemental Diagnosis of Generalist Mobile Manipulation Policies
papers.summary: We present EBench, a simulation benchmark that diagnoses generalist mobile manipulation policies beyond a single success-rate scalar. EBench comprises 26 diverse and challenging manipulation tasks annotated along 5 capability dimensions and 4 generalization dimensions. We evaluate state-of-the-art generalist manipulation models including π_0, π_{0.5}, XVLA, and InternVLA-A1, and reveal that models with near success rates exhibit strikingly different capability profiles: π_{0.5} achieves the highest test success rate and the best train--test retention, whereas InternVLA-A1 dominates mobile mani
